This is the single source of truth for the PoP message format and matches the Telcoin-Network protocol's Rust encoding.","params":{"uncompressedPubkey":"The 192-byte uncompressed G2 BLS public key","validatorAddress":"The validator's execution address"}},"scalarMulG1(bytes,uint256)":{"details":"Scalar multiply the G1 curve point using the `G1_MUL` precompile","returns":{"_0":"_ The resulting G1 point after scalar multiplication"}},"scalarMulG2(bytes,uint256)":{"details":"Scalar multiply the G2 curve point using the `G2_MUL` precompile","returns":{"_0":"_ The resulting G2 point after scalar multiplication"}},"validatePointG1(bytes)":{"details":"Validates an EIP2537-compliant G1 point by adding it to the identity using G1_ADD","returns":{"_0":"_ False if not 128 bytes in length, is not on the curve, or is the identity point"}},"validatePointG2(bytes)":{"details":"Validates an EIP2537-compliant G2 point by adding it to the identity using G2_ADD","returns":{"_0":"_ False if not 256 bytes in length, is not on the curve, or is the identity point"}},"verifyProofOfPossession(bytes,bytes,address)":{"details":"Single entrypoint for consumers (e.g. `ConsensusRegistry`): builds the PoP message, encodes the points to EIP2537 form, hashes to G1, and runs the pairing check, all internally. Mirrors the planned native Rust precompile so the call site is address- and ABI-stable across the migration. The EIP2537 pairing precompile rejects off-curve / wrong-subgroup points, so no separate validation is required here.Parameter order mirrors the protocol's Rust `verify_proof_of_possession_bls(proof, public_key, address)` so this call site is stable when the implementation moves to the native precompile.","params":{"uncompressedPubkey":"192-byte uncompressed G2 public key","uncompressedSignature":"96-byte uncompressed G1 signature (the proof of possession)","validatorAddress":"The validator's execution address bound into the PoP message"},"returns":{"_0":"_ True if the proof of possession is valid"}},"verifyProofOfPossessionG1(bytes,bytes,bytes,bytes)":{"params":{"blsPubkey":"Using G2 group for public keys (256 bytes when EIP2537-encoded)","dst":"The Domain Separator Tag to use for hashing the message to G1","message":"The raw message that was signed (pre-hashing). eg for a PoP: `message = concat(validatorAddress, blsPubkey)`","signature":"Using G1 group for signatures (128bytes when EIP2537-encoded)"}}},"stateVariables":{"B_IN_BYTES":{"details":"Constants for `expand_message_xmd` variant defined in RFC9380"},"EIP2537_FIELD_ELEMENT_SIZE":{"details":"EIP-2537 encoding sizes"},"FIELD_ELEMENT_SIZE":{"details":"Actual size of BLS12-381 field elements in bytes (381 bits = 48 bytes)"},"G1_ADD":{"details":"Relevant BLS12-381 precompiles"},"G1_GENERATOR":{"details":"Standard BLS12-381 Generator Points (EIP-2537 encoded)"},"G1_IDENTITY":{"details":"The BLS12-381 identity elements (infinity/zero points) encoded to comply with EIP2537"},"G1_IDENTITY_RAW":{"details":"The BLS12-381 identity elements in raw uncompressed format (pre-encoding)"},"G2_GENERATOR_NEG":{"details":"Negation of the BLS12-381 `G2_GENERATOR` point, used for signature pairing checks in our PoP"},"HASH_TO_G1_DST":{"details":"The Standard DST for BLS12-381 G1 Basic Signatures (RFC 9380/9677) https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9380#appendix-J.9.1"},"L":{"details":"L parameter from RFC 9380 for hash-to-field operations guarantees proper statistical distribution in hash-to-field operations L = ceil((ceil(log2(p)) + k) / 8) where: - p is the BLS12-381 field modulus (381 bits) - k is the security parameter (128 bits) - L = ceil((381 + 128) / 8) = ceil(509 / 8) = 64 bytes"},"P":{"details":"381-bit base field prime modulus"},"POP_INTENT_PREFIX":{"details":"Fixed prefixes for the proof-of-possession message, matching the Telcoin-Network protocol's Rust encoding. `POP_INTENT_PREFIX` differentiates BLS proof intents; `ADDRESS_LEN_PREFIX` signals the 20-byte validator address length encoding. See `proofOfPossessionMessage()`."}},"title":"BlsG1 Proof of Possession Library","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"G1_GENERATOR()":{"notice":"These are the standard generators, useful for deriving public keys from secrets in tests"},"HASH_TO_G1_DST()":{"notice":"This is the generic default used by `blst::min_sig`. Integrators should consider custom DST."},"decodeG2PointFromEIP2537(bytes)":{"notice":"Reverses the coordinate reordering (x.c0/c1 -> x.c1/c0) used in encoding"},"encodeG2PointForEIP2537(bytes)":{"notice":"Coordinate reordering is performed because EIP2537 precompiles expect: `x.c0 || x.c1 || y.c0 || y.c1` This differs from some bls libraries (such as blst used by TN) which serialize to `x.c1 || x.c0 || y.c1 || y.c0`"},"expandMessageXmd(bytes,bytes,uint16)":{"notice":"Expands a message using the expand_message_xmd method as per RFC9380::5.3.1 https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9380#name-expand_message_xmd"},"hashToField(bytes,bytes,uint256)":{"notice":"Extension degree of field `m = 1` thus simpler `sgn0_m_eq_1(x)` applies"},"proofOfPossessionMessage(bytes,address)":{"notice":"Builds the canonical proof-of-possession message a validator signs: `POP_INTENT_PREFIX || uncompressedPubkey || ADDRESS_LEN_PREFIX || validatorAddress`."},"verifyProofOfPossession(bytes,bytes,address)":{"notice":"Verifies a validator's BLS12-381 proof of possession from raw uncompressed inputs."},"verifyProofOfPossessionG1(bytes,bytes,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"Provided parameters must be uncompressed and EIP2537-compliant due to BLS12-381 precompile limitations Using G2 group for public keys (256bytes for EIP2537) and G1 group for signature (128bytes for EIP2537) Validators must sign over `prefixA || BLS pubkey || prefixB || validatorAddress` to ensure possession Where `prefixA = POP_INTENT_PREFIX && prefixB = ADDRESS_LEN_PREFIX` (constants inserted by rust protocol encoding)**Verifies a Proof of Possession for a generic message using a custom DST"}},"notice":"Utility to perform proof of possessions for BLS12-381 using I2OSP(uint256,uint256).details: Convert nonnegative integer to its big-endian byte representation as per RFC8017::4.1 https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8017#section-4.1

params.xLen: Length for the output octet byte array

addG1(bytes,bytes).details: Adds two G1 curve points using the G1 addition precompile

addG2(bytes,bytes).details: Adds two G2 curve points using the G2 addition precompile

decodeG1PointFromEIP2537(bytes).details: Decodes an EIP2537-encoded G1 point (128 bytes) to uncompressed format (96 bytes)

decodeG2PointFromEIP2537(bytes).details: Decodes an EIP2537-encoded G2 point (256 bytes) to uncompressed format (192 bytes)

eip2537BytesToFieldElement(bytes,uint256.details: Extracts a 48-byte BLS12-381 field element from EIP2537 64-byte padded format

params.dest: The destination byte array to write the 48-byte element
params.destOffset: The offset in the destination to start writing
params.paddedElement: 64-byte EIP2537 padded field element (16 zeros + 48 bytes)
params.paddedOffset: The offset in the padded byte array to start reading

encodeG1PointForEIP2537(bytes).details: Encode a BLS12-381 G1 point (signature for min_sig) to EIP2537 format (128 bytes)

returns._0: _ EIP2537-compliant, 128-byte encoded G1 point comprising two 64-byte padded field elements (x, y coordinates)

encodeG2PointForEIP2537(bytes).details: Encode a BLS12-381 G2 point (public key for min_sig) to EIP2537 format (256 bytes)

returns._0: _ EIP2537-compliant, 256-byte encoded G1 point comprising four 64-byte padded field elements (x, y coordinates)

params.dst: The domain separation tag as string of at most 255 bytes
params.outputLen: The desired output length of the expanded message bytes

extractBytes(bytes,uint256,uint256).details: Extract a slice of bytes from a larger byte array

params.offset: Starting position in the source array

fieldElementToEIP2537Bytes(bytes,uint256.details: Pads a 48-byte BLS12-381 field element to 64 bytes with 16 leading zeros for EIP2537

params.dest: Destination bytes to write the padded element.
params.destOffset: Offset in dest where the 64-byte padded element should start, meaning the bytes in range `dest[destOffset:destOffset + 16]` must be zero!
params.source: Source bytes containing the field element
params.sourceOffset: Offset in source where the 48-byte field element starts

hashToField(bytes,bytes,uint256).details: Hash input bytes to field element(s) in BLS12-381 base field Fp as outlined by RFC9380::5.2 https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9380#name-hash_to_field-implementation

params.count: The number of field elements to generate
params.dst: Domain separator tag to prevent collisions between different hash usages

hashToG1(bytes,bytes).details: Hash to a point on the G1 curve using hash-to-curve method outlined by RFC9380::8.8.1 https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9380#section-3-4.2.1

returns._0: _ The deterministic point on G1 curve derived from `message`

isInfinityPointG1(bytes).details: Checks if an EIP2537-compliant G1 point is the identity (infinity/zero point)

params.eip2537PointG1: Is not validated to be a G1 point on the curve; this must be enforced separately

isInfinityPointG2(bytes).details: Checks if an EIP2537-compliant G2 point is the identity (infinity/zero point)

params.eip2537PointG2: Is not validated to be a G2 point on the curve; this must be enforced separately

isUncompressedInfinityPointG1(bytes).details: Checks if an uncompressed G1 point (96 bytes) is the identity (infinity/zero point)

isUncompressedInfinityPointG2(bytes).details: Checks if an uncompressed G2 point (192 bytes) is the identity (infinity/zero point)

mapFieldElementToG1(BlsG1.Fp).details: Maps a field element to a point on the G1 curve using the map-to-curve precompile

processFieldElement(bytes,uint256).details: Extract and process a single field element from uniform bytes

params.index: The index of the field element to extract
params.uniformBytes: The uniform random bytes from an `expand_message` schema (we use xmd)

proofOfPossessionMessage(bytes,address).details: Pure message assembly only; on-curve validity is enforced by the pairing check in `verifyProofOfPossession`. This is the single source of truth for the PoP message format and matches the Telcoin-Network protocol's Rust encoding.

params.uncompressedPubkey: The 192-byte uncompressed G2 BLS public key

scalarMulG1(bytes,uint256).details: Scalar multiply the G1 curve point using the `G1_MUL` precompile

returns._0: _ The resulting G1 point after scalar multiplication

scalarMulG2(bytes,uint256).details: Scalar multiply the G2 curve point using the `G2_MUL` precompile

returns._0: _ The resulting G2 point after scalar multiplication

validatePointG1(bytes).details: Validates an EIP2537-compliant G1 point by adding it to the identity using G1_ADD

returns._0: _ False if not 128 bytes in length, is not on the curve, or is the identity point

validatePointG2(bytes).details: Validates an EIP2537-compliant G2 point by adding it to the identity using G2_ADD

returns._0: _ False if not 256 bytes in length, is not on the curve, or is the identity point

verifyProofOfPossession(bytes,bytes,addr.details: Single entrypoint for consumers (e.g. `ConsensusRegistry`): builds the PoP message, encodes the points to EIP2537 form, hashes to G1, and runs the pairing check, all internally. Mirrors the planned native Rust precompile so the call site is address- and ABI-stable across the migration. The EIP2537 pairing precompile rejects off-curve / wrong-subgroup points, so no separate validation is required here.Parameter order mirrors the protocol's Rust `verify_proof_of_possession_bls(proof, public_key, address)` so this call site is stable when the implementation moves to the native precompile.

params.uncompressedSignature: 96-byte uncompressed G1 signature (the proof of possession)
params.validatorAddress: The validator's execution address bound into the PoP message

returns._0: _ True if the proof of possession is valid

params.blsPubkey: Using G2 group for public keys (256 bytes when EIP2537-encoded)
params.dst: The Domain Separator Tag to use for hashing the message to G1
params.message: The raw message that was signed (pre-hashing). eg for a PoP: `message = concat(validatorAddress, blsPubkey)`
params.signature: Using G1 group for signatures (128bytes when EIP2537-encoded)

G1_GENERATOR().notice: These are the standard generators, useful for deriving public keys from secrets in tests

HASH_TO_G1_DST().notice: This is the generic default used by `blst::min_sig`. Integrators should consider custom DST.

decodeG2PointFromEIP2537(bytes).notice: Reverses the coordinate reordering (x.c0/c1 -> x.c1/c0) used in encoding

encodeG2PointForEIP2537(bytes).notice: Coordinate reordering is performed because EIP2537 precompiles expect: `x.c0 || x.c1 || y.c0 || y.c1` This differs from some bls libraries (such as blst used by TN) which serialize to `x.c1 || x.c0 || y.c1 || y.c0`

expandMessageXmd(bytes,bytes,uint16).notice: Expands a message using the expand_message_xmd method as per RFC9380::5.3.1 https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9380#name-expand_message_xmd

hashToField(bytes,bytes,uint256).notice: Extension degree of field `m = 1` thus simpler `sgn0_m_eq_1(x)` applies

proofOfPossessionMessage(bytes,address).notice: Builds the canonical proof-of-possession message a validator signs: `POP_INTENT_PREFIX || uncompressedPubkey || ADDRESS_LEN_PREFIX || validatorAddress`.

verifyProofOfPossession(bytes,bytes,addr.notice: Verifies a validator's BLS12-381 proof of possession from raw uncompressed inputs.

verifyProofOfPossessionG1(bytes,bytes,by.notice: Provided parameters must be uncompressed and EIP2537-compliant due to BLS12-381 precompile limitations Using G2 group for public keys (256bytes for EIP2537) and G1 group for signature (128bytes for EIP2537) Validators must sign over `prefixA || BLS pubkey || prefixB || validatorAddress` to ensure possession Where `prefixA = POP_INTENT_PREFIX && prefixB = ADDRESS_LEN_PREFIX` (constants inserted by rust protocol encoding)**Verifies a Proof of Possession for a generic message using a custom DST
